spec_match.rationale: The page distinguishes declarative manifest migration from manual origin-scoped service worker state transfer, using migrate_to and migrate_from terminology.

interactive.rationale: A runtime probe invokes available CacheStorage checks, detects service worker, postMessage, IndexedDB, Notification, and push surfaces, and updates output from live controls.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept lede now matches the feature framing: installed-app identity is browser-managed after validation, while SW state needs application code.

edge_cases.rationale: Controls cover missing CacheStorage, opaque responses, missing destination frame, notification permission fallback, skipped payloads, and unavailable push support.

references.rationale: The references section now cites chromestatus.com/feature/5123349239955456 and the WICG origin migration spec section.

design_system.rationale: The added runtime controls use shared variables, paired button background/color rules, stable panel dimensions, and no raw hex colours.

summary: Major finding resolved: the service worker bridge now has a real try-it runtime hook and explicit fallback scenarios.
